preamble: The transformation from a lateral approximant to an alveolar flap is a common liquid interchange process involving the loss of lateral airflow, often occurring as an allophonic variation or as a result of liquid dissimilation.

examples.note: Intervocalically, the single liquid phoneme is realized as an alveolar flap.
Dissimilation where the lateral suffix becomes rhotic when the root contains another lateral (e.g., solaris).
Foreign laterals are mapped to the native alveolar liquid, which is primarily a flap.

sources: Sohn, H. M. (1999). The Languages of Korea.
Weiss, M. (2009). Outline of the Historical and Comparative Grammar of Latin.
Vance, T. J. (2008). The Sounds of Japanese.
